findings.evidence: `Cylinder.lean:130` adds `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]`; its proof uses `integral_indicator₀` (`Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Integral/Bochner/Set.lean:185`), `setIntegral_const` (`.../Set.lean:527`), and `blockLaw_blockCylinder` (`Cylinder.lean:73`), none of which require `IsFiniteMeasure μ`.
findings.fix: Remove `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]` from the theorem signature; the same proof should use `integral_indicator₀`, `setIntegral_const`, and `blockLaw_blockCylinder` unchanged.
findings.issue: `integral_blockIndicatorProd` assumes `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]`, but the identity only uses the Bochner indicator integral and `toReal` measure value, so it holds for an arbitrary measure.

summary: The new lemma is at the right rectangle/block-indicator level, but it carries an unnecessary finite-measure assumption.
